# v1.2.0 — OCC 2026-13 Response + Ecosystem Onramps

**Released:** 2026-XX-XX (date set at tag time)
**Sibling parity bar:** cre-agent-audit v0.2.2.dev0 + v1.2 OCC 2026-13 overlay
**Tag:** [v1.2.0](https://github.com/linus10x/finserv-agent-audit/releases/tag/v1.2.0)
**Zenodo DOI:** [pending re-issue at tag time]

---

## What changed since v1.1.0

v1.1 shipped 15 governance modules + 14 FSI regulatory mappings + 12 repo-root governance/release surfaces + 5 vendor-clauses companions + 4 reference integrations + 3 CI lint scripts + mypy `--strict` + a 90% coverage gate. v1.2 ships the **OCC 2026-13 white-space response** (treating the OCC's rescission of Bulletin 2011-12 as the buyer-conversation opener), the **Treasury FS AI RMF** + **NIST AI 600-1 GenAI Profile** regulatory mappings, the first wave of **ecosystem onramps** (MCP server adapter, OpenTelemetry GenAI emitter, GitHub Actions composite action + reusable workflow, CLI tool), the **buyer-conversation closers** (pre-examination self-assessment, Big-4 engagement-letter exhibit, sample evidence pack, CAIO first-90-days playbook), the **maturity model + CLI self-score**, the **drift-detection test pack** (failure-modes-matrix + doc-staleness), and the **`ProtectedClassProxyDetector` real implementation** (closes the v1.1 stub per ADR-0019). The PyPI release pipeline lands at the same time: **Trusted Publishing + PEP 740 Sigstore-attested wheels** via `.github/workflows/publish.yml`.

Maintainer note: this notes file anticipates the full shape of v1.2 across Tranches A-D. Refresh the bullets below from `CHANGELOG.md` `[1.2.0]` once Tranche A-D commits land — only the surfaces that actually shipped should remain in the highlight list at tag time.

## Highlights

### Code

- **`ProtectedClassProxyDetector` real implementation** (closes v1.1 stub per ADR-0019). Mutual-information threshold on `(feature, protected_class)` pairs, HMDA + GiveMeSomeCredit benchmark coverage, published FPR/FNR failure-mode list.
- **MCP server adapter** — exposes governance surfaces (DEFCON state, veto log, audit-chain verification, vendor-score drift status) as Model Context Protocol tools so MCP-compatible orchestrators can read and act on them without a custom client.
- **OpenTelemetry GenAI emitter** — emits the v1.1 `AuditEventType` taxonomy as OTEL spans + attributes aligned to the OTel GenAI semantic conventions; ships with documented Datadog / Grafana / Splunk receivers in `examples/integration/`.
- **CLI tool** — `finserv-audit verify --jsonl PATH` exit-codes 0/1 against any JSONL audit-event stream; designed to drop into CI as a single-command gate.
- **Maturity-model self-score** — `finserv-audit maturity` evaluates the current repo's surface coverage against the 5-level FSI-AI-governance maturity rubric and prints a score + improvement deltas.

### Documentation

- **OCC 2026-13 overlay** (`docs/occ_2026_13_overlay.md`) — the framework's white-space play. Maps every v1.1 governance pattern to OCC's post-rescission guidance posture and the bank-supervisory expectations that survived the rescission (model-risk governance principles continue to apply via SR 11-7 + interagency Statement of Principles).
- **Treasury FS AI RMF mapping** (`docs/treasury_fs_ai_rmf_mapping.md`) — Treasury's Financial Services AI Risk Management framework crosswalked to NIST AI RMF + the 14 v1.1 FSI mapping docs.
- **NIST AI 600-1 GenAI Profile mapping** (`docs/nist_ai_600_1_genai_profile_mapping.md`) — GenAI-specific risk controls (confabulation, dangerous-content generation, data-integrity, IP, obscene/abusive/illegal content, information-security, value-chain integration) mapped to the governance surface.
- **Buyer-conversation closers** (4 new surfaces): `docs/pre_examination_self_assessment.md` (regulator-readiness checklist) · `docs/big_four_engagement_letter_exhibit.md` (drop-in audit-evidence exhibit Big-4 attest teams can attach to an engagement letter) · `docs/sample_evidence_pack.md` (annotated sample of what a clean run of the framework hands a regulator or auditor) · `docs/caio_first_90_days_playbook.md` (Chief AI Officer first-90-days operational sequence).
- **MRM BRIDGE template** (`docs/mrm_bridge_template.md`) — a Model Risk Management bridge artifact reconciling SR 11-7 model-risk lifecycle owners with v1.1 governance gate owners.

### CI + tooling

- **`publish.yml`** — PyPI Trusted Publishing (OIDC, no API tokens) + PEP 740 Sigstore-attested wheels via `pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1`. Two-stage flow: TestPyPI smoke -> required-reviewer gate -> production PyPI. One-time maintainer setup in `docs/PYPI_TRUSTED_PUBLISHING_SETUP.md`.
- **GitHub Actions composite action** (`.github/actions/finserv-audit-verify/`) — adopters call `uses: linus10x/finserv-agent-audit/.github/actions/finserv-audit-verify@v1.2.0` to gate a PR on an audit-stream verify in three lines of YAML.
- **Reusable workflow** (`.github/workflows/finserv-audit-verify.reusable.yml`) — for adopters with org-policy preferences for `workflow_call` over composite actions.
- **Drift-detection test pack** — `tests/test_failure_modes_matrix.py` (drift between `FAILURE-MODES.md` and code) + `tests/test_doc_staleness.py` (drift between `__all__` exports and "v0.X candidate" markers in docs). Both lift to CI-required green status.

### Voice + brand discipline

- Cross-cutting OCC 2011-12 citation updates note the 2026-13 rescission while preserving the historical citation (`docs/occ_2011_12_mapping.md` rebadged "historical reference; supersession context in `docs/occ_2026_13_overlay.md`").
- Banned-term lint surface unchanged; the new docs pass `scripts/banned_term_lint.py` clean.

## Upgrade notes

### Backward compatibility

The v1.1 deprecation re-export shims (`patterns/*`, `schemas/*`, `examples/defcon_state_machine`) are **removed in v1.2** as announced in the v1.1 release notes. Adopters who did not migrate to the canonical `finserv_agent_audit.governance` / `finserv_agent_audit.schemas` paths during the v1.1 deprecation window will hit `ModuleNotFoundError` at import time. Migration is mechanical: change `from patterns.sovereign_veto import SovereignVeto` to `from finserv_agent_audit.governance.sovereign_veto import SovereignVeto`, etc. Maintainer note: confirm with Tranche A subagent reports whether the shim removal actually landed in this tranche before keeping this bullet in the final notes; if it deferred to v1.3, move this bullet to the deferred section below.

### New optional dependencies

The framework's default install remains zero-runtime-dependency. Three new optional extras:

```
pip install finserv-agent-audit[mcp]               # MCP server adapter
pip install finserv-agent-audit[otel]              # OpenTelemetry GenAI emitter
pip install finserv-agent-audit[all-integrations]  # both, plus the v1.1 examples/integration deps
```

The `[mcp]` extra pulls `mcp>=0.5`. The `[otel]` extra pulls `opentelemetry-api`, `opentelemetry-sdk`, `opentelemetry-semantic-conventions-ai` (when published; the emitter degrades to upstream OTel semconv until then).

### CLI entry point

After install:

```
finserv-audit verify --jsonl path/to/audit.jsonl
finserv-audit maturity
```

Exit codes: 0 on pass, 1 on any verification failure or drift.

### CI impact

Adopters mirroring the CI workflow get two new optional steps: the composite action (`uses: linus10x/finserv-agent-audit/.github/actions/finserv-audit-verify@v1.2.0`) and the reusable workflow (`uses: linus10x/finserv-agent-audit/.github/workflows/finserv-audit-verify.reusable.yml@v1.2.0`). Both are opt-in.

The maintainer's own CI gains the two drift tests (`test_failure_modes_matrix.py` + `test_doc_staleness.py`) inside the existing `pytest` step; no separate job.

## Manual follow-ups for the maintainer

1. **PyPI Trusted Publishing setup** — execute the one-time setup in `docs/PYPI_TRUSTED_PUBLISHING_SETUP.md` before the v1.2.0 tag push. The first tag push will fail without it.
2. **Zenodo DOI re-issue** — link the new tag to the existing Zenodo record so the DOI badge resolves to v1.2.0.
3. **Refresh this release-notes body** — replace placeholder bullets with the actual shipped surface once Tranches A-D commits land. Anything not shipped in this tranche moves to the "deferred to v1.3" list.
4. **README badges** — add the Sigstore-signed badge and the PyPI version badge once the first production publish completes.

## What's deferred to v1.3 — Discrimination Frontier + Vendor Surface

- **Discrimination-frontier patterns** — LDA search for protected-class proxies beyond mutual-information, LLM-as-classifier disparate-impact harness, effective-challenge harness, customer-facing chatbot guardrail
- **Vendor-clauses foundation-model class** — sixth vendor class covering foundation-model API providers (OpenAI / Anthropic / Google / AWS Bedrock / Azure OpenAI)
- **NYDFS Part 500 AI mapping** — New York Department of Financial Services cybersecurity regulation, AI overlay
- **State-AG enforcement matrix** — multi-jurisdiction state attorney-general AI-enforcement actions catalog
- **Retraining-cadence monitor** — drift-trigger-based retraining recommendation gate
- **Deprecation-watch** — model + vendor deprecation calendar with sunset-date assertions
- **Vendor-attestation ledger** — append-only ledger of vendor attestations (model cards, eval reports, incident notices)
- **Disclosure templates** — adverse-action, model-use, vendor-AI disclosure templates
- **Incident-retrospective template** — post-incident write-up template aligned to NIST AI RMF GOVERN-6.2

## What's deferred to v2.0 — Agentic-AI Ecosystem + Platform Surfaces

- **Agentic-AI ecosystem adapters** — Google A2A · LangGraph · Microsoft Agent Framework (MAF) · CrewAI
- **AIBOM generator** — AI Bill of Materials per the emerging CISA + EU specs
- **FastAPI governance endpoint** — REST surface exposing DEFCON state, veto log, audit-chain verification
- **Kubernetes operator** — DEFCON as a Kubernetes custom resource with controller-driven escalation
- **Adversarial test pack** — red-team scenarios per ADR-0018 threat model
- **PE portfolio playbook** — operating-partner-facing rollout sequence for portco AI governance
- **NAIC insurance mapping** — NAIC Model Bulletin on Use of AI Systems by Insurers
